deep_dive.title: SSD: Spatially Speculative Decoding Accelerates Autoregressive Image Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Autoregressive models excel in visual generation by treating images as 1D sequences of discrete tokens, mirroring language modeling. However, this flattening discards the intrinsic 2D spatial locality of visual signals, creating severe computational bottlenecks during inference. We introduce Spatially Speculative Decoding (SSD), a framework that aligns the predictive objective with the natural geometry of images. Rather than predicting only the immediate next token in a 1D sequence, our model simultaneously predicts the adjacent horizontal token and the token directly below it. By capitalizing on this 2D spatial correlation, spatially speculative decoding overcomes the memory wall in visual inference. Our approach accelerates autoregressive image generation by up to 13.3x while maintaining high fidelity on DPG-Bench and GenEval. Our results suggest that respecting the underlying geometry of vision unlocks massive computational efficiencies, paving the way for real-time, high-resolution autoregressive generative models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: speculative decoding for autoregressive image generation in VLM
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 对视觉语言模型中自回归图像生成应用投机解码
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 对视觉语言模型中自回归图像生成应用投机解码
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 对视觉语言模型中自回归图像生成应用投机解码
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Spatially Speculative Decoding (SSD) that leverages 2D spatial locality to predict multiple tokens simultaneously, significantly accelerating autoregressive image generation while maintaining quality.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出空间投机解码（SSD）框架，利用图像的二维空间相关性，在预测当前标记的同时预测相邻标记，从而加速自回归图像生成。该方法突破了将图像展平为一维序列导致的存储墙问题，实验表明在不牺牲生成质量的前提下显著降低推理延迟。该工作首次将投机解码有效应用于视觉生成领域，为高效VLM推理提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出空间投机解码（SSD）框架，利用图像的二维空间相关性，在预测当前标记的同时预测相邻标记，从而加速自回归图像生成。该方法突破了将图像展平为一维序列导致的存储墙问题，实验表明在不牺牲生成质量的前提下显著降低推理延迟。该工作首次将投机解码有效应用于视觉生成领域，为高效VLM推理提供了新范式。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: speculative decoding for vision language model

deep_dive.title: Forget Without Compromise: Nexus Sampling for Streaming KV-Cache Eviction Under Fixed Budgets
deep_dive.abstract: Long-context and agentic LLM workloads push the KV cache past any fixed memory budget, forcing the inference stack to permanently evict tokens at every step of a continuous-inference stream. Existing methods all share the same template, a per-step direct-attention score followed by deterministic top-$K$ selection, which converts a single below-cutoff step into an irreversible verdict and permanently erases any subtly important token that direct attention cannot single out from noise. To address this challenge, we propose Nexus Sampling, a training-free eviction method that pairs Nexus scoring, an iterative walk over direct attention that surfaces bridge tokens, with weighted reservoir sampling, which retains tokens with inclusion probability in place of deterministic top-$K$. Theoretically, we show that Nexus Sampling dominates deterministic top-$K$ in long-run survival of subtly important tokens. Empirically, at 80% KV cache eviction, Nexus Sampling matches dense attention within 1% on LongBench while outperforming top-$K$ baselines on retrieval-heavy tasks, with up to 10x smaller per-sequence cache memory.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache eviction method for long-context LLM inference efficiency
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对长上下文大语言模型推理的KV缓存驱逐方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对长上下文大语言模型推理的KV缓存驱逐方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对长上下文大语言模型推理的KV缓存驱逐方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Nexus Sampling, a training-free KV-cache eviction method that uses iterative attention scoring and weighted reservoir sampling to retain important tokens under fixed budget, improving long-context LLM inference efficiency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出Nexus Sampling，一种无需训练的KV缓存驱逐方法，通过迭代注意力评分和加权水库采样，在固定预算下保留重要标记，从而提升长上下文大语言模型的推理效率。该方法解决了确定性驱逐策略下重要标记被永久删除的问题，实验表明在保持性能的同时有效压缩缓存。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出Nexus Sampling，一种无需训练的KV缓存驱逐方法，通过迭代注意力评分和加权水库采样，在固定预算下保留重要标记，从而提升长上下文大语言模型的推理效率。该方法解决了确定性驱逐策略下重要标记被永久删除的问题，实验表明在保持性能的同时有效压缩缓存。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.

deep_dive.title: Spectral Evolution-Guided Token Pruning in Multimodal Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Reducing visual token redundancy is critical for accelerating Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) without degrading cross-modal reasoning performance. Existing token pruning methods typically rely on single-layer signals, such as attention scores or token similarities, which overlook the cross-layer transformation of visual representations and may exhibit positional bias in multimodal token sequences. To address this limitation, we propose a training-free token pruning framework based on Cross-Layer Spectral Evolution (CLSE). Instead of measuring token importance from single-layer feature magnitudes, CLSE quantifies how token representations evolve across Transformer layers in the frequency domain. This evolution reflects the transition from high-frequency structural details to low-frequency semantic abstractions. We observe that tokens with stronger spectral redistribution across layers are more likely to be semantically active and should therefore be preserved. By modeling cross-layer token dynamics, CLSE provides a stable importance criterion that mitigates positional bias. Extensive experiments on both image and video benchmarks demonstrate that CLSE achieves a superior trade-off between efficiency and accuracy under aggressive token reduction. Across multiple MLLMs, CLSE reduces FLOPs, KV cache memory, and latency while maintaining competitive or improved performance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Token pruning to accelerate multimodal LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过token剪枝加速多模态大模型推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过token剪枝加速多模态大模型推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过token剪枝加速多模态大模型推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a training-free token pruning framework for MLLMs using cross-layer spectral evolution to reduce visual token redundancy and accelerate inference without degrading performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多模态大语言模型中视觉token冗余导致推理速度慢的问题，提出基于跨层频谱演化的免训练token剪枝框架。通过量化token表示在Transformer层间的频率域演化，而非单层特征幅度，来准确识别冗余token。实验表明该方法在不损失推理性能的前提下显著加速多模态模型推理，为VLM高效推理提供了一种轻量级解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对多模态大语言模型中视觉token冗余导致推理速度慢的问题，提出基于跨层频谱演化的免训练token剪枝框架。通过量化token表示在Transformer层间的频率域演化，而非单层特征幅度，来准确识别冗余token。实验表明该方法在不损失推理性能的前提下显著加速多模态模型推理，为VLM高效推理提供了一种轻量级解决方案。
